About the Author

### Kyle Bonn

Senior Content Producer Kyle Bonn is a Syracuse University broadcast journalism graduate with over a decade of experience covering soccer globally. Kyle specializes in soccer tactics and betting, with a degree in data analytics. Kyle also does TV broadcasts for Wake Forest soccer, and has had previous stops with NBC Soccer and IMG College. news_items.title: Preview: USA vs Paraguay - prediction, team news, lineups | World Cup 2026 - Sports Mole
news_items.text:
Preview: USA vs Paraguay - prediction, team news, lineups | World Cup 2026 - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 1

Los Angeles Stadium

# Preview: USA vs Paraguay - prediction, team news, lineups | World Cup 2026

By Jonathan O'Shea| 10 Jun 2026 15:55, Last updated: 11 Jun 2026 12:36

© Imago / IMAGO / Icon Sportswire

Commencing their Group D campaign at World Cup 2026, the USA and Paraguay will look to make a positive start when they meet in Los Angeles on Friday.

In a group also featuring Australia and Turkey, both the co-hosts and their South American counterparts know only a top-two finish will guarantee progress to the knockout phase.

---

## Match preview

One of three nations co-hosting this summer's World Cup, the United States will join neighbours Mexico and Canada in hoping to make the most of home advantage.

Mauricio Pochettino's side were spared the effort of CONCACAF qualifying, giving the Argentinian coach almost two years to plan and prepare for the tournament, but each match will matter from now on.

Soon after posting an impressive 3-2 win over Senegal, their final warm-up game ended in defeat last weekend, as they lost 2-1 to Germany despite Antonee Robinso

news_items.title: USMNT's Mauricio Pochettino won't say if Chris Richards can start World Cup opener, but his presence is welcome | Sporting News
news_items.text:
USMNT's Mauricio Pochettino won't say if Chris Richards can start World Cup opener, but his presence is welcome | Sporting News

Continue Without Login

Featured

Sports

Explore More

Editions

# USMNT's Mauricio Pochettino won't say if Chris Richards can start World Cup opener, but his presence is welcome

Mike DeCourcy

•

Add The Sporting News on Google

INGLEWOOD, Calif. – As of Thursday afternoon LA time, United States men's national team head coach Mauricio Pochettino knew the identity of the 11 players he will send out to start the World Cup 2026 opener Friday evening against Paraguay. And eventually, you will, too.

And in between, the players will find out.

Probably.

The man they call Poch was giving away no secrets on matchday-minus-1 at SoFi Stadium, when he addressed the media in the customary press conference. This was particularly true with respect to central defender Chris Richards, the 2025 U.S. Soccer Male Player of the Year and quite possibly the most irreplaceable individual on the USMNT.

“I think we celebrate that at the start of the competition we have 26 players available to be selected,” Pochettino said. “He is available to be selected, and then we will d

news_items.title: United States receives enormous injury boost on eve of FIFA World Cup - The Mirror US
news_items.author: Sahil Kurup
news_items.text:
# United States receives enormous injury boost on eve of FIFA World Cup - The Mirror US

Author: Sahil Kurup
Published: 2026-06-12T04:08:12+00:00
Source: themirror.com (themirror.com)
Language: en

## Story

United States receives enormous injury boost on eve of FIFA World Cup - The Mirror US

# United States receives enormous injury boost on eve of FIFA World Cup

## Mauricio Pochettino confirmed on Thursday that Chris Richards is available to face Paraguay in the World Cup opener, declaring all 26 players fit

Comments

Sahil Kurup U.S. Sports Reporter

00:08 ET, 12 Jun 2026Updated 00:12 ET, 12 Jun 2026

View 3 Images

U.S. Men's National Team defender Chris Richards has been dealing with an ankle injury since May(Image: Omar Vega/Getty Images)

The United States have received the news they needed most on the eve of their World Cup opener, with coach Mauricio Pochettino confirming on Thursday that Chris Richards is available to face Paraguay at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood on Friday night.

‌

Richards, widely considered the team's most important central defender, has spent the past three weeks racing to recover from a significant ankle injury sustained while playing for Crystal Pal

# Preview: USA vs Paraguay - prediction, team news, lineups | World Cup 2026

By Jonathan O'Shea| 10 Jun 2026 15:55, Last updated: 11 Jun 2026 12:36

© Imago / IMAGO / Icon Sportswire

Commencing their Group D campaign at World Cup 2026, the USA and Paraguay will look to make a positive start when they meet in Los Angeles on Friday.

In a group also featuring Australia and Turkey, both the co-hosts and their South American counterparts know only a top-two finish will guarantee progress to the knockout phase.

---

## Match preview

One of three nations co-hosting this summer's World Cup, the United States will join neighbours Mexico and Canada in hoping to make the most of home advantage.

Mauricio Pochettino's side were spared the effort of CONCACAF qualifying, giving the Argentinian coach almost two years to plan and prepare for the tournament, but each match will matter from now on.

Soon after posting an impressive 3-2 win over Senegal, their final warm-up game ended in defeat last weekend, as they lost 2-1 to Germany despite Antonee Robinso"}

# USMNT's Mauricio Pochettino won't say if Chris Richards can start World Cup opener, but his presence is welcome

Mike DeCourcy

•

Add The Sporting News on Google

INGLEWOOD, Calif. – As of Thursday afternoon LA time, United States men's national team head coach Mauricio Pochettino knew the identity of the 11 players he will send out to start the World Cup 2026 opener Friday evening against Paraguay. And eventually, you will, too.

And in between, the players will find out.

Probably.

The man they call Poch was giving away no secrets on matchday-minus-1 at SoFi Stadium, when he addressed the media in the customary press conference. This was particularly true with respect to central defender Chris Richards, the 2025 U.S. Soccer Male Player of the Year and quite possibly the most irreplaceable individual on the USMNT.

“I think we celebrate that at the start of the competition we have 26 players available to be selected,” Pochettino said. “He is available to be selected, and then we will d"}

# United States receives enormous injury boost on eve of FIFA World Cup

The United States have received the news they needed most on the eve of their World Cup opener, with coach Mauricio Pochettino confirming on Thursday that Chris Richards is available to face Paraguay at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood on Friday night.
